2000-05-01.title: Manchester Journal; Later Pub Hours? Europe Tells Britain It's Time
2000-05-01.abstract: Manchester Journal on proposal by British Home Office to simplify nation's liquor licensing laws for first time in more than 80 years, bringing Britain into line with rest of Europe by allowing some pubs to serve alcohol 24 hours per day; under current law, pubs are required to close at 11pm six days and 10:30 on Sunday; reaction in Manchester, where change is supported by pub owners, customers and police noted; map; photo (M)

2000-05-01.title: After Visit, Congressman Wouldn't Halt Iraq Sanctions
2000-05-01.abstract: Repr Tony P Hall, first American member of Congress to visit Iraq to assess impact of economic sanctions, says while some of suffering he saw is 'heartbreaking' he does not support lifting of embargo, imposed following invasion of Kuwait in 1990; releases report calling for greater US attention to problems of country's population; says Iraq still poses military threat and that until government of Pres Saddam Hussein cooperates in eliminating weapons of mass destruction as required by UN, lifting of sanctions would be irresponsible (S)

2000-05-01.title: Battle for Land in Kenya Has Deep, Bitter Roots
2000-05-01.abstract: Focus on land distribution problem in Kenya, where five percent of people in population of 27 million are squatters; even though British colonialists set stage for crisis, source of current problem remains unclear 37 years after independence; many sqautters blame own leaders; note while there are still white farmers, majority have sold out or gone into business with African elite who have ties to Pres Daniel arap Moi--one of nation's largest landowners and one of its richest men; while few experts believe Kenya is teetering around violence that has punctuated Zimbabwe's land crisis, anger persists; clashes between police and squatters on British-owned farm near Nairobi who have threatened to take land by force discussed; photos; map (M)

2000-05-01.title: Jews Are Caught Up In the Turmoil in Iran
2000-05-01.abstract: Thirteen Jews are to go on trial in Iran's Revolutionary Court, accused of spying for Israel. Jewish groups and human rights organizations have condemned the case as a political show trial aimed at frustrating President Khatami's attempts to develop warmer relations with some of the country's traditional enemies, including the United States.    Article, Page 12.

2000-05-01.title: Iran's Jewish Minority Caught Up in Turmoil
2000-05-01.abstract: Focus on plight of Iran's Jewish minority, which has survived in City of Shiraz for past 2,700 years but currently finds itself squeezed in conflict between hard-line Muslim hierarchy that controls judiciary and reformers backing Pres Mohammad Khatami's efforts at liberalization; local Jewish leaders are troubled by upcoming trial before Revolutionary Court of 13 of their number, who are accused of spying for Israel, calling it worst event in anyone's memory to befall dwindling minority; condemn case, along with human rights officials, as political show trial aimed at frustrating Khatami's attempts to develop warmer relations with some of country's traditional enemies, including US; photo (M)

2000-05-02.title: Listvyanka Journal; Siberian Delicacy: Fish With an Indelicate Smell
2000-05-02.abstract: Siberians consider omul, fish caught in Lake Baikal, delicacy; up to 150,000 tons of omul live in lake in Listvyanka, Russia; numbers have declined in recent years, and government limits annual take to 10,000 tons; map; photos (M)

2000-05-02.title: Pope's Labor Rally Joins Mass and Rock Concert
2000-05-02.abstract: Pope John Paul II presides over May Day rally outside Rome that combines Catholic Mass with rock concert dedicated to third world debt relief; photo (M)

2000-05-02.title: First of Iranian Spy Suspects Is Tried and Confesses on TV
2000-05-02.abstract: Hamid Tefileen, first of 13 Jewish men accused of espionage, goes on trial in Iran; authorities display him on television admitting that he had spied for Israel; Tefileen confesses after government refuses to allow family members, human rights groups or public to witness opening of trials; says he was part of network of spies who were trained in Israel; decision to conduct espionage trial behind closed doors has drawn criticism from human rights groups and diplomats; map; photo (M

2000-05-02.title: Pham Van Dong, Voice of Vietnam's Revolt, Dies at 94
2000-05-02.abstract: Pham Van Dong, who led Vietnamese Communists' delegation to Geneva peace talks in 1954 that ended French war in Indochina and then served as Hanoi's prime minister for three decades, dies at age 94; photo (M)

2000-05-02.title: World Briefing
2000-05-02.abstract: EUROPE    IRELAND: ORANGEMEN CANCEL  The Orange Order has canceled a May 28 march in Dublin that would have been the Protestant group's first there since 1937. Ian Cox, an Orange official, said he took the action because the order, widely viewed as anti-Catholic, had received ''abusive letters and phone calls'' and city officials had ''abandoned'' the organizers. The order holds hundreds of parades in Northern Ireland in the annual ''marching season'' that began last week.    Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-05-03.title: Arrest May End Slovakia Ex-Premier's Influence
2000-05-03.abstract: Violent arrest by commandos of Vladimir Meciar, former Prime Minister of Slovakia, may finally end his extraordinary influence over Slovak politics; he governed Slovakia from independence in 1993 when it split from Czech Republic until September 1998, when he was defeated by coalition of four parties; his government was widely considered illiberal, discriminatory and corrupt, practicing crony politics; photo (M)

2000-05-03.title: Russian Labs Could Divert U.S. Funds, Report Says
2000-05-03.abstract: Report by General Accounting Office concludes that American support could inadvertenely help Russian scientists and former weapons labs make germ weapons, even as Clinton Administration seeks increase in money for Russian-American biological cooperation; its findings discussed (M)

2000-05-03.title: Sophistication of Chinese Counterfeits Makes Them Harder to Detect : Can You Tell the Fake From the Real?
2000-05-03.abstract: Counterfeiters in southern China are producing copies of 2,000-year-old pottery soldiers, horses and dancing ladies with such skill and sophistication these days that experts say they are finding it increasingly difficult to detect the fake from the real.

2000-05-03.title: Jerusalem Journal; More Than One Way to Remember the Holocaust
2000-05-03.abstract: Deborah Sontag's Jerusalem Journal on shunning of Holocaust Day observance in Israel by ultra-Orthodox Jews who believe mainstream account of event overemphasizes physical heroism and martrydom of nonreligious Jews who resisted Nazis and regard observance as secular event, dictated by secular authorities; photo (M)

2000-05-03.title: Administration Steps Up Effort For Support of China Trade Bill
2000-05-03.abstract: Clinton Administration steps up pressure on Congress to pass decisive China trade measure granting it membership in World Trade Organization, acknowledging it still lacks votes for passage; begins final push to persuade undecided members of Congress; argues that bill is as much about global leadership and fate of Chinese reformers as about interests of American business; national security adviser Samuel Berger says rejecting permanent normal trade relations will be worst possible blow to best hopes in 30 years of encouraging positive change in China; Pres Clinton warns rejection would be serious threat to national security; Administration officials and Congressional supporters estimate they are 10 votes shy of 218 needed for majority approval in House; opposition of labor unions, which have subjected lawmakers to intensive lobbying campaign, noted; photo (M)

2000-05-04.title: Crisis Grips Sri Lanka As Guerrillas Gain in North
2000-05-04.abstract: The Sri Lankan cabinet took emergency steps tonight to save money to support the war effort at the northern tip of this island nation, where government forces are battling Tamil rebels -- and the rebels are winning.    The Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam, a guerrilla group that is notorious for its suicide bombers, today lobbed mortar fire into a strategic military base on Sri Lanka's northern peninsula in the latest phase of a powerful offensive.

2000-05-04.title: To Aid Trade Bill, Democrat Creates Plan for Rights Panel
2000-05-04.abstract: With supporters of a China trade bill scrambling for every vote, a Michigan Democrat in the House is planning to introduce legislation this week that he predicts will rescue the Clinton administration's top remaining foreign policy goal from potential defeat.     The legislator, Sander M. Levin, said today that he is putting the final touches on a proposal to establish a commission with powers to review human rights, labor policies and development of the rule of law in China. After weeks of test-marketing in Congress, Mr. Levin said his proposal for parallel legislation to the trade bill could recruit enough fence-sitting Democrats to tip the balance in favor of the administration.

2000-05-04.title: 2 Trials Focus Attention on Iranian Justice
2000-05-04.abstract: In a packed courtroom covered with flowery oriental carpets, two smiling young men told a judge today that they had decided to shoot one of the country's leading reformers because they believed that he was corrupt and an enemy of Islam.    The trial, in which eight men are charged in the attempted assassination of the reformer, Saeed Hajjarian, an editor and political strategist close to President Mohammad Khatami, has mesmerized this city.

2000-05-04.title: Birthrate Dips in Ex-Communist Countries
2000-05-04.abstract: The collapse of Communism in 1989 produced a sharp drop in the fertility rate throughout Eastern and Central Europe that could reduce the region's population nearly 20 percent by the year 2050, according to a United Nations report issued today.    With political collapse and economic uncertainty, many women almost immediately stopped having children or decided to delay motherhood, according to the report by the United Nations Economic Commission for Europe, part of a larger economic survey of Europe.

2000-05-04.title: American Topics : Court-Ordered Shame Makes a Comeback
2000-05-04.abstract: Rebecca Escobar, who killed a woman in a drunken-driving accident, was sentenced to walk around the county courthouse in Wilkesboro, North Carolina, one day a month, holding a sign that reads, "I am a convicted drunk driver, and as a result I took a life." Richard Gorosh, a Michigan podiatrist convicted of health-care fraud, was ordered to advertise his guilty plea in two newspapers. In several cities, officials place derogatory signs before decrepit buildings to embarrass slumlords.

2000-05-05.title: More U.N. Troops Held
2000-05-05.abstract: The number of United Nations peacekeepers held hostage by rebels in the West African nation of Sierra Leone rose to at least 92, including 23 soldiers from an Indian battalion. Secretary General Kofi Annan flew back to New York from Paris to decide on the next step in the crisis.    Article, Page A14.

2000-05-05.title: Detected in Asia, Computer Affliction Moved Swiftly and Crippled Big Systems : 'Love' Virus Hits E-Mail Worldwide
2000-05-05.abstract: NEW YORK—  An e-mail-based virus devastated computers around the world Thursday, stealing passwords, interrupting communications and destroying files for hundreds of thousands of users from the House of Commons in Britain to Ford Motor Co.

2000-05-05.title: Tension Builds Between Iran's 2 Camps
2000-05-05.abstract: After a roller-coaster two weeks that saw a harsh crackdown on reformists, many Iranians have settled into a watchful uncertainty.    The 16 reform publications shut by judges remain silent, and trials continue of intellectuals. The suspense is now focused on what other means the guardians of the rigid Islamic government may use to try to trim the power of younger forces pushing for a more open democracy.

2000-05-05.title: Sri Lanka Lists Tough New Controls on Speech and Property
2000-05-05.abstract: Sri Lanka government, after series of humbling military defeats at hands of Tamil rebels, goes on 'war footing;' announces that authorities will now be able to confiscate private property, shut newspapers and prohibit public meetings, strikes and criticisms of president, if doing so is deemed necessary to national security (M)

2000-05-05.title: Israel and Guerrillas Exchange Reprisals
2000-05-05.abstract: In reprisal for the fiercest cross-border attack since last June, Israeli warplanes bombarded two power stations and a guerrilla arms depot in Lebanon this morning.     The cross-border attack, which occurred on Thursday evening, was itself retaliatory. A spate of casualties in southern Lebanon prompted Shiite Muslim guerrillas to fire five barrages of Katyusha rockets into northern Israel, killing one Israeli soldier and sowing panic in the northern border town of Qiryat Shemona.

2000-05-06.title: Montreal Journal; No Longer Fading, City Booms Back Into Its Own
2000-05-06.abstract: Montreal has staged comeback after years of fray and decay; it has reinvented itself as center for biotech, telecommunications and information technology companies; city, with 3.3 million people, is now third most popular site in North America for conventions; photos (M)

2000-05-06.title: Town, Awaiting Peace, Reels From Congo War
2000-05-06.abstract: Residents of Kananga, Congo, still reeling from war, are overjoyed that their city has been designated one of four outposts of United Nations mission that will arrive sometime this year; human cost of war is evident everywhere in Kananga, which has no electricity, water for only hour per week and rusting remains of great rail hub; Kananga has been free of fighting, but it has become place of refuge for others with terrible stories to tell; thousands of emaciated refugees seeking escape from endless war are nearby; map (M)

2000-05-06.title: Lockerbie Trial Hears Police Minutiae, Defense Hints and, at Day's End, List of Dead
2000-05-06.abstract: Police officers who searched scenes of crash of Pan Am 103 in Lockerbie, Scotland, in 1988 take stand in trial of two Libyans accused of blowing up plane; defense lawyers try in vain to get witnesses to say that CIA and FBI agents had many opportunities to plant or steal evidence at crash site (M)

2000-05-06.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-05-06.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: BLAIR VOWS COOPERATION -- Prime Minister Tony Blair promised to work with Ken Livingstone, the left-wing maverick he tried to keep off the ballot but who won the race for London's first elected mayor with 58 percent of the final vote. Mr. Blair also acknowledged that Conservative success in winning back some 600 of the 3,300 local council seats across England represented ''some bad results.'' But Conservatives suffered a shock loss of a parliamentary seat in the one by-election, in Romsey, southeast England, where the winner was a Liberal Democrat.    Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-05-06.title: Britain and Ireland Agree On a New Start for Ulster
2000-05-06.abstract: British and Irish governments announce agreement on new formula to restart suspended power-sharing government in Belfast and they urge Ulster's Roman Catholic and Protestant leaders to endorse it; new plan envisages resumption on May 22 of government that was suspended in February; it sets new date of June 2001 for putting in place all steps of flagging Northern Ireland peace accord; outline of new blueprint indicates that governments have dropped demand for disarmament itself and instead seek declaration from Irish Republican Army and Protestant paramilitary groups that they would rule out acts of violence in future (M)

2000-05-07.title: Zambian Criticizes Leadership of Sierra Leone Peacekeepers
2000-05-07.abstract: Zambian Pres Frederick Chiluba says that 200 Zambian peacekeepers taken hostage in Sierra Leone were deployed too quickly into unknown territory, with out-of-date maps and shortage of vital equipment; map (S)

2000-05-07.title: Russian Official Tries to Ensure Rights in Chechnya
2000-05-07.abstract: Vladimir Kalamanov, chief of Russian human rights commissionappointed by Pres Vladimir Putin, has task for persuading skeptical West that Russia is serious about ending human rights abuses in Chechnya; interview; photo (M)

2000-05-07.title: Rise in Cases of Mad Cow Disease Alarms Europe
2000-05-07.abstract: European officials are alarmed by steady rise in cases of mad cow disease, despite strict preventive measures that were ordered in 1996; numbers are still miniscule compared with 178,000 cases in Britain, but disease has now been found in native-born cows in ten European countries; photo of French cattle breeder Guy Jambon whose entire herd was slaughtered after one cow was found diseased; as many as 50,000 of France's 21 million cattle are to be examined each year; European Commission wants other member countries to join Britain and France in taking strictest precautions, and plans regional monitoring system (M)

2000-05-07.title: Voice of Hope in Kosovo Tells the U.S. of Goals
2000-05-07.abstract: Veton Surroi, publisher of Kosovo's largest newspaper who is considered voice of reason in traumatized region, discusses his hopes of shaping more civil society despite skepticism of international officials now running province; Surroi, in Washington to receive award from National Endowment for Democracy, mocks UN fumbles; interview; photo; argues that Kosovo can have own constitution and define its borders within republic of Yugoslavia, and suggests it is too soon to push for independence (M)

2000-05-07.title: Japan Faces A Free-for-All If Top Faction Loses Control
2000-05-07.abstract: Japan's longtime style of factional politics could change in aftermath of Prime Min Keizo Obuchi's incapacitating stroke and retirement of his mentor, Noboru Takeshita (S)

2000-05-08.title: Falling Euro, a Unity Currency, Instead Bolsters Dollar and Pound
2000-05-08.abstract: Europe's common currency, or euro, which was supposed to unify economies of 11 European countries and be symbol of stability to rival dollar, yen and even British pound, has instead been boon to currencies with which it was supposed to compete; has declined steadily in value since introduction in early 1999, from $1.17 to 89 cents in recent days; decline has mystified analysts, mortified politicians, alarmed central bankers and reignited suspiciuons among those who never liked idea of giving up currencies (M)

2000-05-08.title: Your Own Computer Network, on a Pay-as-You-Go Basis
2000-05-08.abstract: PARIS—  Smartpartners Ltd., an umbrella organization for several technology consultancies spread throughout Britain, had a seemingly modest goal. "We wanted to share information within our own office," said Leslie Franks, communications director.

2000-05-08.title: FIGHTING RESUMES NEAR THE CAPITAL OF SIERRA LEONE
2000-05-08.abstract: Sierra Leone edged back toward war today as government and rebel forces fought each other in a town not too far from the capital here, and the United Nations force that is supposed to keep a peace between them sank deeper into disarray.    Panic spread through Freetown early today after the United Nations force mistakenly announced that rebels of the Revolutionary United Front were advancing on the capital by using human shields and stood less than 15 miles from this seaside capital. Even after the United Nations retracted its announcement in the predawn hours, the streets of Freetown remained tense through the day.

2000-05-08.title: East Asia Unites to Fight Speculators
2000-05-08.abstract: CHIANG MAI, Thailand—  A broad grouping of East Asian nations, including Japan, South Korea and China, agreed this weekend to work toward curbing economic turmoil in the region by defending one another's currencies from speculative attack.

2000-05-08.title: Rwanda Says It Is Willing to Begin Withdrawal From Congo
2000-05-08.abstract: Rwanda says it is ready to begin gradual withdrawal of troops from Congo, step members of UN Security Council say could be first real opening for negotiations to end two-year-old war that currently involves six African nations; map (M)

2000-05-09.title: Gunmen Fire on Protesters in Sierra Leone
2000-05-09.abstract: Thousands of protesters, including rival militias, march on compound of rebel leader Foday Sankoh in capital of Freetown throwing rocks; his partisans retaliate by firing into crowd; reportedly shoot some protesters execution-style; at least four die; photos (M)

2000-05-09.title: Gunmen Kill a Former Leader of the Kosovo Liberation Army
2000-05-09.abstract: Unidentified gunmen kill Ekrem Rexha, well-known commander of disbanded Kosovo Liberation Army outside home in southern town of Prizren; death comes three weeks after killing of Besim Mala, former KLA commander and hero, during argument with business partner (M)

2000-05-09.title: Year Later, China's New View of U.S. Bombing
2000-05-09.abstract: Chinese are channeling anger resulting from NATO bombing of Embassy in Belgrade year ago into strengthening of economy and there is little sentiment for renewed protests; Government, which railed against US, is focused on passage of trade bill by Congress which would give country membership in World Trade Organization; many Chinese regard measure as litmus test of American attitudes (S)

2000-05-09.title: Calling Novel an Insult to Islam, Students in Cairo Battle Police
2000-05-09.abstract: Police in Cairo, Egypt, clash with students protesting 17-year-old novel they charge insults Islam; novel Banquet of Seaweed by Syrian writer Haider Haider was first published in Cyprus in 1983; was reprinted by Egypt's Ministry of Culture in 1999 to promote major works of modern Arabic literature (S)

2000-05-09.title: World Briefing
2000-05-09.abstract: EUROPE    NORTHERN IRELAND: PROTESTANT OPTIMISM  A key Protestant politician predicted that the Ulster Unionist Party would accept the Irish Republican Army's offer to open its secret arms arsenal to international inspection as a basis for agreeing to re-enter the Northern Ireland home rule government with Sinn Fein, the I.R.A.'s political allies. John Taylor, the unionists' deputy leader, said the I.R.A.'s announcement opened the way for resuming the Belfast government by May 22.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-05-10.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-05-10.abstract: EUROPE    BRITAIN: BLAIR DEFIANT  In his first speech since his most significant setback in three years in office, Prime Minister Tony Blair said he would not change his government's program to suit his critics. ''Come fair weather or foul we intend to stick to the course we have set out,'' he said in an uncompromising address to the Periodical Publishers' Association. In elections last week, Mr. Blair suffered the loss of nearly 600 local council seats and the indignity of seeing Ken Livingstone, whom he opposed, become London's first elected mayor.   Warren Hoge (NYT)

2000-05-10.title: Investigators Locate More Money Believed Looted From Nigeria
2000-05-10.abstract: Investigators seeking funds looted from Nigeria during rule of late Sani Abacha have found $630 million in Luxembourg bank accounts linked to his sons; Luxembourg prosecutor says accounts have been frozen at bank, M M Warburg & Co; probe has embarrassed Swiss officials and set off probe of Credit Suisse (M)

2000-05-10.title: Bethlehem Journal; For Palestinians, O Little Town of Business Plans
2000-05-10.abstract: Palestinian Development and Investment Co (Padico), fastest growing Palestinian business conglomerate, holds annual meeting, Bethlehem; Palestinian Legislative Council Speaker Ahmed Qurei hails Padico for imposing private sector style on Palestine's development; investors are cross-section of emerging Palestinian business elite; biggest investor, Saudi Prince al-Waleed bin Talal, is one of few non-Palestinians and does not attend; company has turned autonomous Palestinian territory into most privatized economy in Mideast; map; photo (M)

2000-05-10.title: A Doomed Peace: Missteps and a Weak Plan Marred Effort for Sierra Leone
2000-05-10.abstract: Analysis of flaws in United Nation's Sierra Leone peacekeeping mission; flaws reach back to rush to end civil war and send in international force to patch together what some call 'peace on the cheap;' peacekeeping force was formed from hodgepodge of nations whose armies had never worked together and was given minimal equipment; United States, Britain and European nations refused to supply troops (M)

2000-05-10.title: Power Array Pushes the China Trade Bill
2000-05-10.abstract: Former Presidents Gerald R Ford and Jimmy Carter and array of top officials from Democratic and Republican administrations rally with Pres Clinton and Vice Pres Al Gore at White House to urge Congress to approve bill to grant China permanent normal trading privileges; Clinton intensifies his lobbying in advance of House vote (M)

2000-05-11.title: Clinton Issues Order to Ease Availability of AIDS Drugs in Africa
2000-05-11.abstract: Pres Clinton issues executive order making AIDS drugs available far more cheaply throughout sub-Saharan Africa, against wishes of United States pharmaceutical industry; executive order declares that United States government will not seek to interfere with countries in that region that violate American patent law in order to provide AIDS drugs at lower prices; order extends to most of Africa special arrangement provided last year to South Africa; it allows African countries to either license local comapnies to produce generic versions or import drugs from third countries where they are available at lower cost (M)

2000-05-11.title: Joining Sri Lanka's Sword: Might of Censor's Pen
2000-05-11.abstract: Sri Lanka's chief censor, Ariya Rubasinghe, is purging criticism of government's faltering war on separatist rebels from newspapers, radio and television; government imposed what journalists there say is harshest censorship of press ever; it also banned public rallies and trade union strikes that are deemed contrary to national security; previous governments in Sri Lanka have also clamped down during country's 17-year war with Liberation Tigers, but new regulations are more far-reaching and include harsh new penalties; photo (M)

2000-05-11.title: Thousands Flee to Sierra Leone Capital as War Looms
2000-05-11.abstract: Fighting in Sierra Leone edges closer to capital, and thousands of panicky men, women and children flee outlying towns, carrying loads on their heads or scrambling to board jammed buses; as confusion intensifies, whereabouts of Foday Sankoh, rebel leader, remain unclear; his absence complicates efforts to negotiate release of 500 United Nations peacekeepers and civilians being held by rebels; United Nations mission, whose mandate has been to keep peace as neutral force, has essentially become allied with Sierra Leonean army and militia known as Kamajors to stave off rebel assault on capital, Freetown; photo (M)

2000-05-11.title: Sri Lankan Rebels Renew Assault on Jaffna
2000-05-11.abstract: Separatist rebels in Sri Lanka reject government offer of cease-fire and resume their offensive, striving to take back Jaffna peninsula (M)

2000-05-11.title: Russia's New Prime Minister: A Tested Economic Liberal
2000-05-11.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin names Mikhail M Kasyanov as his prime minister; Kasyanov is chief foreign-debt negotiator and liberal economics expert; experts say his appointment bodes well for efforts to tame Russia's feral style of capitalism; Kasyanov persuaded private Western creditors in February to agree to reduction of nearly $32 billion in Russian debt; photo (M)

2000-05-12.title: Bill to Expand Fabric Imports From Africa Clears Senate
2000-05-12.abstract: Senate votes 77 to 19 to approve bill to promote commerce with Africa and Caribbean, ending more than five years of defeats on trade bills for Clinton administration; vote reflects unusual bipartisan support for compromise measure worked out by Senate and House negotiators that would lower tariffs and quotas on products from sub-Saharan Africa, Caribbean and Central America; most of measure's provisions will not take effect for several months, until trade officials determine which countries and products are eligible for new privileges (M)

2000-05-12.title: Sierra Leone Rebels Halted Near Capital but Active Elsewhere
2000-05-12.abstract: Forces loyal to government of Sierra Leone appear to push back rebels attacking capital of Freetown, but fighting intensifies and spreads to other areas; coalition of United Nations peacekeepers, Sierra Leone soldiers and militia known as Kamajors secures road leading to Waterloo, 18 miles from Freetown, but heavy fighting breaks out east of there; in Freetown, pro-government forces tighten security, mindful that infiltrators played critical role last year in rebel attacks that nearly overran capital; photo of refugees fleeing Freetown; map (M)

2000-05-12.title: Rebels Destroy a Convoy Outside Chechnya
2000-05-12.abstract: Islamic rebels mount major attack outside Chechnya's borders for first time in nearly 9 months, nearly wiping out convoy of 22 Russian soldiers moving down mountain road in neighboring region of Russia, Ingushetia; ambush kills 18 Russian troops and wounds 3 others; Russian planes later bomb forests near ambush site; map (M)

2000-05-12.title: Russian Security Agencies Raid Media Empire's Offices
2000-05-12.abstract: Federal agents wielding guns and wearing black masks stage raid on Moscow headquarters of Media-MOST, Russia's largest private media empire, raising alarms among politicians and journalists about prospects for independent press under pres Vladimir V Putin; Media-MOST controls influential radio and television stations as well as newspaper, news magazine and other publications; top law enforcement officials issue conflicting statements on purpose of criminal probe; Vladimir Gusinsky, president and founder of Media-MOST, says raid is meant as political pressure; photo (M)

2000-05-12.title: Corrections
2000-05-12.abstract: Correction of May 9 map with article about renaming of German army base for German sergeant who saved hundreds of Jews in Lithuania in World War II and was executed by Nazis, regarding geographical description

2000-05-13.title: U.N. CHIEF FAULTS RELUCTANCE OF U.S. TO HELP IN AFRICA
2000-05-13.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan, faced with wars on several fronts in Africa, says peacekeeping efforts need kind of military help that US is now unwilling to provide; sees little room for US role in new style of peacekeeping, given American reluctance to put troops on the ground; Annan, who would like to send in sophisticated military teams to assess problems and set up missions, says it is disheartening to confront continent with calamities from end to end, and consider what this says about African leadership; interview; photo; map (M)

2000-05-13.title: On Road Again in Midwest, Clinton Promotes China Trade
2000-05-13.abstract: Pres Clinton takes campaign to improve China trade ties to Midwest, standing on hay bales in Minnesota to predict that soybean and corn producers could see 'almost incalculable' increase in exports if China opens its markets; Clinton earlier stops in Canton for panel discussion on trade (M)

2000-05-13.title: Opposition Grows to Deadline for Pulling G.I.'s From Kosovo
2000-05-13.abstract: Pentagon and bipartisan group of senators criticize legislation that sets deadline for withdrawing US ground troops from Kosovo, warning that measure would undermine NATO peacekeeping and embolden Serb leader Slobodan Milosevic (M)

2000-05-13.title: Head Scarf Required at Official Functions : For Malaysian Women, Pressure to Be Islamic
2000-05-13.abstract: KUALA LUMPUR—  Rafidah Aziz, the trade and industry minister of Malaysia, wears bright, floral outfits and is famous for her stinging tongue and fierce negotiating tactics.

2000-05-13.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-05-13.abstract: ASIA    INDONESIA: ACEH TRUCE -- The government and representatives of separatist rebels from Aceh Province signed an agreement described as a ''humanitarian pause,'' resulting in the first cease-fire in more than two decades. While both sides welcomed the truce, signed at an undisclosed location in Geneva, as a milestone, both sides were quick to qualify it. The rebels pledged to continue pushing for independence, and Jakarta ruled out recognizing the insurgents. The cease-fire will begin June 2.    Elizabeth Olson (NYT)

2000-05-14.title: Rising Internet Use Quietly Transforms Way Japanese Live
2000-05-14.abstract: Internet is quietly transforming Japan, making celebrities out of on-line farmers like Rei Kimura, empowering women, changing way people apply for jobs and schools, and chipping away at traditional behavior patterns; country has voracious appetite for cruising cyberspace using mobile terminals, and may be next year be world's largest Internet user; Internet has given voice to groups with little influence, allowing farmers like Kimura to escape huge bureaucratic distribution system that sucks up their profits; photo; map; working mothers are setting up joint business ventures, small companies are decreasing reliance on corporate customers, and employees are able to seek new opportunities without asking permission of bosses (M)

2000-05-14.title: Art Dynasty's Suit Over Reports of Wartime Dealings Is Rejected
2000-05-14.abstract: Paris appeals court rejects damage claim brought by Wildenstein family, New York-based dynasty of art dealers, against Hector Feliciano, American writer who has described business dealings between French-Jewish family and Nazi officials during French occupation; book alleges that late Georges Wildenstein had business contacts with Nazi art dealer Karl Habrstock (M)

2000-05-14.title: Sierra Leone Rebel Leader Reportedly Smuggled Gems
2000-05-14.abstract: Documents collected in Sierra Leone and obtained by The New York Times show that warlord Foday Sankoh has systematically exploited diamonds to pay for his rebellion and enrich himself; papers were found in Sankoh's looted house (M)

2000-05-14.title: Putin Decrees New Controls And Envoys For Provinces
2000-05-14.abstract: Pres Vladimir Putin moves to assert control over Russia's far-flung and often headstrong provinces; orders 89 provinces reorganized into seven vast administrative districts, each overseen by powerful Kremlin representatives answerable only to Putin (S)

2000-05-14.title: Sierra Leone's Slide Back to Warlord Rule Draws In U.N.
2000-05-14.abstract: Sierra Leone, state in name only, slides back into rule by warlords ensconced in different regions, each with access to enough diamond mines to buy arms; nine-month-old peace accord collapses and Pres Ahmed Tejan Kabbah is rendered powerless by rise of warlords like Foday Sankoh and Johnny Paul Koroma; photos; map; difference now is that UN peacekeepers are in midst of chaos (M)

2000-05-15.title: Rebel Group in Sierra Leone Frees 18 U.N. Peacekeepers
2000-05-15.abstract: 18 UN peacekeepers among more than 500 held hostage by rebel forces in Sierra Leone since beginning of May are released; heavy fighting reported between pro-government forces in Masiaka, 35 miles east of capital of Freetown (S)

2000-05-15.title: International Traveler / Update : Strike Empties French Cash Machines
2000-05-15.abstract: PARIS—  A nationwide strike by cash couriers continued through the weekend, leaving automated teller machines empty of cash and sending consumers and small businesses scurrying to find change.

2000-05-15.title: Social Democrats Win Re-election in Germany's Largest State
2000-05-15.abstract: Governing Social Democrats win comfortable victory in North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany's largest state, confirming both resurgence of Chancellor Gerhard Schroder and plight of his opposition foes; wins approximately 43.5 percent of vote, compared with 37 percent for Christian Democrats; state's Social Democratic premier, Wolfgang Clement, will remain in office (M)

2000-05-15.title: German Urges Europe Create Central Body For Governing
2000-05-15.abstract: German Foreign Minister Joschka Fischer, in direct challenge to European nation states, proposes creation of European federation with directly elected president and parliament sharing real executive and legislative powers; says he is expressing himself in personal capacity; appeal for foundation of federation that would shift power from national governments to central European administration comes at time when 15-member European Union is pondering how to manage expansion(M)

2000-05-15.title: Seoul Faces Dilemma on Missile Issue in North-South Talks
2000-05-15.abstract: SEOUL—  U.S. and South Korean officials reportedly are at odds on how hard President Kim Dae Jung should press the issue of North Korea's missiles and nuclear research when he meets the North Korean leader, Kim Jong Il, in Pyongyang next month for the first North-South Korean summit talks.

2000-05-16.title: No Inquiry on Gulf Attack
2000-05-16.abstract: Army says it has no plans to reopen an investigation into an attack made at end of Persian Gulf war in 1991 by soldiers under command of Gen Barry R McCaffrey, who is currently Pres Clinton's drug czar (S)

2000-05-16.title: U.N.'s Unlikely Ally for Sierra Leone
2000-05-16.abstract: Release of 139 UN peacekeepers by rebels in Sierre Leone was facilitated agency's unlikely ally, Pres Charles Taylor of Liberia, former warlord who terrorized population into making him president; Taylor has been for years principal ally of Foday Sankoh, leader of Sierra Leone's Revolutionary United Front, and has allowed rebel group to smuggle diamonds through Liberia and made arms available to it; map; photo (M)

2000-05-16.title: Pollution Victims Start to Fight Back in China
2000-05-16.abstract: Increasing number of Chinese farmers whose livelihoods have been destroyed by water pollution are fighting back with lawsuits seeking compensation; although China has enacted strict antipollution laws in past decade, enforcement is still lax in many places in country's whose rivers are among dirtiest in world; aggressive central government campaign to identify and punish polluters has made big difference in some cities and other places where it has focused, but has failed to penetrate fully vast rural areas, where laws are often ignored by local enterprises and officials; map; photos (M)

2000-05-16.title: Key Democrat Seems to Lean In Support Of Trade Bill
2000-05-16.abstract: Interview with US Repr Charles B Rangel, ranking member of House Ways and Means Committee who signals he may be leaning in favor of decisive China trade bill to grant China entry into World Trade Organization; queries why organized labor is using legislation as litmus test on human rights; says factor in his decision will be how China vote affects labor's support for Democrats in November elections; pressure mounts on uncommitted House members (M)

2000-05-16.title: Palestinian Forces Exchange Gunfire With Israeli Troops
2000-05-16.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian forces exchange gunfire in West Bank as violent protest sweeps through Palestinian enclaves; gun battle near Ramallah and chaotic confrontations throughout West Bank and Gaza described; Israeli forces suppress Palestinians throwing stones and firebombs with tear gas, rubber-coated bullets and sometimes live ammunition; three Palestinians reportedly die and six Israeli soldiers are wounded; protests mark anniversary of 'nabka,' Palestinian term for catastrophe, which they say accompanied birth of Israel; are also calling for release of Palestinian prisoners held by Israel and expressing deep-seated frustrations among many, especially with failure of 1993 Oslo peace agreement to change lives; Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat confer by telephone; Barak in face of violence wins Parliament's approval to cede three villages near Jerusalem to Palestinian Authority in effort to advance peace talks; photos; map (M)

2000-05-17.title: Bush Opposes G.O.P. Bill On Kosovo
2000-05-17.abstract: Gov George W Bush says bill backed by Senate Republicans to set deadline for withdrawing American ground troops from Kosovo would tie his hands if he became president; Sens Trent Lott and John W Warner say they will push ahead with bill, which would cut off funds for troops by July 2001; Republicans seek graceful way out of showdown; Defense Sec William S Cohen says bill would send dangerous signal (M)

2000-05-17.title: Cherie Blair V. Tony Blair, You Might Say
2000-05-17.abstract: Cherie Blair, lawyer and wife of Prime Min Tony Blair, goes to court as Trades Union Congress chief counsel to challenge way his government has introduced parental leave rules into British law; holds rules, adopted under European Union directive, should apply to parents of children now under 5, not just children born or adopted after Dec 15, 1999; Prime Min Blair has ruled out taking leave to be with their baby when it is born; photo (M)

2000-05-17.title: Shorter Presidential Term Gains in France
2000-05-17.abstract: Proposal to cut president's term of office to five years from seven is gaining support from political leaders on both right and left in France; proposal has become hot issue with introduction by ex-Pres Valery Giscard d'Estaing of bill calling for shorter term; Pres Jacques Chirac has opposed change in past, but may shift stance because shorter term would make his age less of issue in re-election bid (M)

2000-05-17.title: Barak Focus Is Long View
2000-05-17.abstract: Analysis of Prime Min Ehud Barak's success in defusing potential crisis in wake of gun battles between Palestinian and Israeli troops; violence is tamped down, although low-level clashes continue in West Bank; back-channel peace talks in Stockholm continue, while Dennis Ross, American peace envoy, meets separately with Barak and Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat; Barak's government, which seemed on verge of collapse, is still intact, though not quite harmonious; absence of sense of crisis reflects Barak's and Arafat's reluctance to be dragged into new cycle of violence; some Palestinians laud Barak for showing commitment to peace effort by winning Parliament's approval for turning over three villages to Palestinian Authority (M)

2000-05-17.title: U.S. Seeks Sweeping Overhaul To Fix Peacekeeping by U.N.
2000-05-17.abstract: Richard C Holbrooke, American ambassador to United Nations, calls for sweeping overhaul of UN peacekeeping operations and more money from member nations to make operations effective; calls for significantly expanding peacekeeping department and allowing Sec Gen Kofi Annan more flexibility in staffing it; Cyprus, Estonia, Israel, Hungary and Philippines respond with offers to pay more; Holbrooke says he hopes to see at least four or five more nations making such offers (M)

2000-05-18.title: In Bush's Words: 'Join Together in Making China a Normal Trading Partner'
2000-05-18.abstract: Excerpts from remarks by Gov George W Bush on need for normal trade relations with China (M)

2000-05-18.title: Prize to Times Writer
2000-05-18.abstract: Steven Erlanger of The New York Times wins award for European reporting from German Marshall Fund of the United States; Erlanger is one of few American journalists to have spent almost all of last year in war zones of Serbia and Kosovo (S)

2000-05-18.title: Seeing Threats Beyond Arms, President Urges Cooperation
2000-05-18.abstract: Pres Clinton tells graduating class at United States Coast Guard Academy in New London, Conn, that congressional vote for extending trade preferences to China will have deep implications for American security; says 'no' vote to trade would invite future of dangerous confrontation and constant insecurity; photo (M)

2000-05-18.title: Serbian Government Seizes TV Station in a Drive to Crush the Opposition to Milosevic
2000-05-18.abstract: Serbian government seizes control of main opposition television station, accusing it of advocating uprising; opposition politicians call immediate protest rally and talk of beginning of outright dictatorship under Pres Slobodan Milosevic; rally attracts 20,000 demonstrators in Belgrade, even though opposition media could not broadcast word of it; seized television station, Studio B, is controlled by Serbian Renewal Movement of Vuk Draskovic; its seizure is attack on him; he is said to be in Montenegro, for his safety; photo (M)

2000-05-18.title: 2 CONGRESS PANELS STRONGLY ENDORSE CHINA TRADE BILL
2000-05-18.abstract: Key House and Senate committees overwhelmingly endorse Clinton administration's China trade bill, contributing to growing sense among backers that measure is headed for passage; half-dozen previously uncommitted House Republicans and Democrats say they will support legislation, necessary step for American companies to benefit fully from deal reached by United States and China that paves China's way into World Trade Organization; bill comes to vote next week; United States trade representative, Charlene Barshefsky, says situation is still fluid, even though House Ways and Means Committee and Senate Finance Committee have now signed off on bill; photo (M)

2000-05-19.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-05-19.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    IRAN: REFORMERS' MAJORITY -- Iran's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, intervened hours before the hard-line Guardian Council was to decide whether to void the results in Tehran parliamentary elections held last February because of fraud. He advised the council only to invalidate boxes where it found discrepancies but to endorse the election of those candidates whose victories were certain. Pro-reform candidates won 29 of Tehran's 30 seats, giving reformers a majority in the 290-seat Parliament.    Nazila Fathi (NYT)

2000-05-19.title: Frederick Coggan, an Archbishop of Canterbury, Dies at 90
2000-05-19.abstract: Frederick Donald Coggan, former archbishop of Canterbury and the first spiritual head of the Church of England to support the ordination of women, dies at age 90; photo (M)

2000-05-19.title: Recife Journal; A Brazilian City Resurrects Its Buried Jewish Past
2000-05-19.abstract: Excavation and reconstruction of first synagogue built in New World is under way in Recife, Brazil; synagogue flourished in mid-1600's when Dutch controlled that part of Brazil; when Portuguese returned, Recife's Jews left for Manhattan, where they founded New York City's Jewish community; small Jewish community left in Recife plans to turn site into museum and Cultural Center of Jewish Presence in Brazil, with support of Brazilian government and several foundations; photos; map (M)

2000-05-19.title: Expecting Fraud, Peru Challenger Drops Out of Presidential Runoff
2000-05-19.abstract: Alejandro Toledo, outspoken challenger in Peru's presidential race, says he is pulling out of runoff election because he believes vote will be fraud; Toledo asks Pres Alberto K Fujimori to delay vote to mid-June, so international election monitors can test computer systems to ensure clean vote; but Toledo does not promise to participate even then unless he receives equal time on main television stations; Fujimori says he will not change election day; Toledo's withdrawal apparently will enable Fujimori to win third five-year term in voting on May 28; his expected victory would cap campaign tainted by international observers' complaints of dirty tricks and vote tampering in first round; should Toledo and Fujimori hold firm, Clinton administration and Organization of American States would be put in difficult position of having to validate uncontested re-election victory or reject it because of irregularities; decision to reject election could lead to economic and political sanctions for Peru; photos (M)

2000-05-19.title: Europeans Learn They're Growing Genetically Altered Plants
2000-05-19.abstract: Farmers in England, France, Germany and Sweden find they unwittingly planted rapeseed oil crops for two years from bags of Canadian seed that inadvertently contained scattering of genetically modified seeds; Europeans are opposed to use of genetic engineering, and newspapers there are referring to fields as tainted and contaminated; European governments say there is no health risk from oil produced from crop, noting that less than 1 percent of seeds had bioengineered genes; newspapers are calling for seed company, Advanta of Netherlands, to pay for destroyed crops (M)

2000-05-20.title: A Year After Victory, Barak Fights on Many Fronts
2000-05-20.abstract: Week of Palestinian violence ends with clashes in West Bank and Gaza and intensified fighting in southern Lebanon, as Israeli Prime Min Ehud Barak marks his first year in office; photo of wounded Israeli soldier; Barak may cancel US trip, although back-channel peace talks are continuing in Sweden (S)

2000-05-20.title: Guatemalan Squatters Torching Park Forests
2000-05-20.abstract: Guatemalan peasants are setting fires to clear forest land in El Ceibal national park, which is home to Mayan ruins but is protected only on paper from squatters and loggers who have cut down best trees; photo; map; region is shrouded in smoke during spring dry season; more than 200 squatters have lived since 1998 in shacks in two settlements in park; unending internal migration affects Peten region, which includes environmentally sensitive jungles of Maya Biosphere Reserve, rich with wildlife, forests and majestic temples; population has increased from 20,000 in 1960 to almost half million; government is trying to fight slash-and-burn fires all around country (M)

2000-05-20.title: Solitary Republican Senator Blocks Peacekeeping Funds
2000-05-20.abstract: New Hampshire Republican Sen Judd Gregg has singlehandedly blocked US from paying $368 million owed to UN for four difficult peacekeeping missions; dispute that infuriates Clinton administration is latest skirmish in battle with Congress on using US power and money abroad in operations short of war; Gregg as chairman of Appropriations Subcommittee has stopped State Department from transferring money already approved by Congress for missions in Congo, East Timor, Kosovo and Sierra Leone; administration warns that delay hinders missions that are already frayed; Gregg, interviewed, objects most to Sierra Leone, saying he will not finance unethical 'barbaric' policy there; reference is to amnesty for rebels who have terrorized and mutilated civilians and held peacekeepers hostages; Gregg photo (M)

2000-05-20.title: Serb Opposition Leaders Going to Moscow to Ask Putin's Help
2000-05-20.abstract: Serb opposition leaders emerge from private meetings and tell rally of fewer than 5,000 people that they will fly to Moscow to ask Russian help in moderating policies of Pres Slobodan Milosevic; gesture comes three days after government seizes Studio B television station and silences independent broadcasting, including radio B2-92; Milosevic's wife's party prepares anti-terrorism law that is expected to be used against opposition (M)

2000-05-20.title: Taiwan's New Leader Ends Decades of Nationalist Rule
2000-05-20.abstract: Chen Shui-bian is sworn in as Taiwan president, ending fifty years of rule by National Party, much of it under martial rule; photo with predecessor Lee Teng-hui; Chen repeats pledges not to declare independence unless Taiwan is attacked, but does not offer commitment to 'one China' as Beijing demands (M)

2000-05-21.title: Swiss Refusal Of Citizenship To Immigrants Raises Debate
2000-05-21.abstract: Rejection of 48 naturalization applications in Emmen, a suburb of Lucern, stirs debate across Switzerland and charges that local government's vote on issue amounted to racist selection; all those turned down are Turks or people from former Yugoslavia; Emmen vote makes clear that foreigners must virtually become more Swiss than the Swiss--pass what the media call the 'fondue test'--to stand a chance of holding a passport (M)

2000-05-21.title: As Political Crisis Mounts, Peru's Leader Relishes Fight
2000-05-21.abstract: Pres Alberto K Fujimori appears eager for fight as he confronts possibility of unending protest marches in Peru, criticism from Washington and other foreign capitals, and even, perhaps, economic sanctions from US and Organization of American States; Alejandro Toledo's withdrawal from May 28 ruonoff presidential election leaves Fujimori with an open field for victory and prospect for a third term, but one in which his legitimacy and credibility would be questioned at home and abroad; photo; map (M)

2000-05-21.title: Armed Gangs Sabotaging Uneasy Peace In Indonesia
2000-05-21.abstract: Armed men terrorize village of Janthoe Baru in Indonesia's Aceh Province, looting, razing houses and forcing people to sleep nights at local mosque; Pres Abdurrahman Wahid concedes that rogue soldiers from Indonesian Defense Forces might be involved in the attacks, but local police and military officials say an unidentified 'third force' of hired thugs, bandits or even disgruntled independence fighters from Free Aceh Movement may be to blame (M)

2000-05-21.title: Mbeki's Visit To U.S. Puts AIDS Activists In a Quandary
2000-05-21.abstract: Pres Thabo Mbeki of South Africa will not be pressed too hard during his visit to US on his pronouncement that AZT is too dangerous to give to HIV-infected pregnant women and his appointment of an AIDS advisory panel that includes researchers who have postulated that HIV does not cause the disease; Clinton administration has urged restraint on AIDS activists and those who treat AIDS, fearing that Mbeki will dig in harder if he is pressured; some scientists hope government will arrange a meeting with prominent researchers so that Mbeki can be put on right path (M)

2000-05-21.title: Former Critics Of Trade Bill Are Swaying Vote in House
2000-05-21.abstract: Perhaps two dozen congressmen who were undecided support China trade bill after Reps Sander M Levin and Doug Bereuter win backing from House leaders to amend bill to establish a human rights and trade policy monitoring commission for China; Levin, a Democrat, has voted against all of Pres Clinton's major trade initiatives (M)

2000-05-22.title: President Views Success of China Trade Bill as His Foreign Policy Legacy
2000-05-22.abstract: Pres Clinton sees coming vote in Congress on granting China membership in World Trade Organization as probably the last hope of making deep imprint on foreign affairs in his second term; has reportedly confided to close friends and colleagues that he will not repeat past mistakes and will not allow politics of moment or even prospect of war within his own political party to pull him back from step he is convinced is best hope of keeping China from becoming adversary (M)

2000-05-22.title: Student Group Emerges As Major Milosevic Foe
2000-05-22.abstract: New student movement demanding sweeping political change is surging in popularity and has become significant target for attack by government of Yugoslav president, Slobodan Milosevic; movement, called Otpor, or Resistance, has given fresh energy, insouciance and arrogant innocence to Milosevic's political opposition; even so, it shows few signs of becoming serious revolutionary organization since it is loosely organized and lacks clear leadership structure; photo (M)

2000-05-22.title: U.S. Did Little to Deter Buildup as Ethiopia and Eritrea Prepared for War
2000-05-22.abstract: Clinton administration, which courted governments of Eritrea and Ethiopia over past two years in efforts forge peace settlement, never sought arms or economic sanctions against them, nor did it seek to slow down more than $1 billion in World Bank loans in same period while both countries prepared for war; focused instead on intensive and sometimes highly personal diplomatic maneuver, in which Pres Clinton personally communicated with two leaders; three diplomats who headed peace effort acknowledge they were reluctant to chastise Prime Min Meles Zenawi of Ethiopia and Pres Isaias Afwerki of Eritrea, who were seen as friends of US and had promise of reformers despite drive to fight each other; the two countries, among poorest on earth, spent $1 billion on arms over past two years (M)

2000-05-22.title: OBITUARY : Robert MacDonald, Ex-Publisher of the IHT, Dies at 69
2000-05-22.abstract: PARIS—  Robert MacDonald, 69, publisher of the International Herald Tribune during its transformation from a money-losing journal for Americans to a global newspaper distributed to an international elite, died Friday of cancer in Sarasota, Florida.

2000-05-22.title: Referendum on 2-Party Plan in Italy Fizzles
2000-05-22.abstract: Ambitious referendum aimed at reducing instability in Italy's electoral system is invalidated after too few Italians turn out for vote; approximately 32.1 percent of voters cast ballots, far less than 50.1 percent quorum needed; most contentious of the seven questions before voters would have moved Italy to closer to two-party sytem by ridding it of parliamentary seats that are allotted proportionally (S)

2000-05-23.title: Rebels Free Half of Force From the U.N. In Africa War
2000-05-23.abstract: Rebels in Sierra Leone release 29 more hostages or nearly half of 500 UN peacekeepers who were seized; officials voice optimism releases will continue in coming days; rebels call for release of leader Foday Sankoh, raising concern that they may continue to hold troops while bargaining for his freedom; photo (S)

2000-05-23.title: U.N. Cool to Increase
2000-05-23.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan recommends to Security Council that agency not increase size of its peacekeeping force in Lebanon until Israel withdraws and Lebanon guarantees safety of troops (S)

2000-05-23.title: U.N. Aide in Kosovo Faults NATO on Unexploded Bombs
2000-05-23.abstract: Boy is killed and two other children seriously wounded by cluster bomb in Kosovo, Serbia; UN official blames NATO for delay in removal of unexploded bombs; since refugees returned to Kosovo in June 1999, more than 100 people have died and hundreds have been wounded in mine and bomb accidents; estimated 40 percent have been victims of cluster bombs (M)

2000-05-23.title: Groups Fear Exclusion From the U.N.
2000-05-23.abstract: UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan, in hopes of dissuading disparate opponents of globalization from demonstrating against millennium assembly in September, tells gathering of independent groups that solution to global inequalities is not confrontation but cooperation with international organizations; praises private agencies and advocacy groups for successful work on social issues and urges them to join hands with him and business leaders to rectify economic wrongs (S)

2000-05-23.title: Roissy Journal; Pilots Just Say Non to English-Only
2000-05-23.abstract: Attempts by Air France to make its pilots speak only English to air traffic controllers at Charles de Gaulle Airport near Paris lasts only 15 days following storm of protests by controllers; carrier says it underestimated both difficulty of such conversion and stubborn Gallic pride that still persists among French pilots and air controllers; map; photo (M)

2000-05-24.title: Temporary Coalition Against Trade Bill Crosses Party and Ideological Lines
2000-05-24.abstract: House members on right and left are banding together in unusual coalition that crosses party and ideological lines to fight China trade bill; some Democratic bill opponents says Republicans are wary of working with Rep David E Bonior, chief Democratic vote-counter against bill; photo (M)

2000-05-24.title: THE HOUSE OPENS A SPIRITED DEBATE OVER CHINA TRADE
2000-05-24.abstract: House opens spirited debate over bill to grant China permanent normal trading relations; undecided members demand wide range of favors for districts, leaving bill's supporters confident they will eke out victory; opponents hold vote is still too close to call; some dozen Democrats announce their support for bill; focus shifts to undeclared Republicans; photos (M)

2000-05-24.title: Germans Plan To Trim Army And Rely Less On the Draft
2000-05-24.abstract: Defense Min Rudolph Scharping accepts recommendations of government-appointed independent commission to drastically reform and shrink draft-based army still set up to fight cold war; stops short of endorsing all-professional army; apparently plans to cut armed forces by about 22 percent, not 30 percent commission recommends (M)

2000-05-24.title: United States and Indonesia Quietly Resume Military Cooperation
2000-05-24.abstract: Clinton administration has quietly resumed military cooperation with Indonesia suspended following massacres in East Timor; plans first joint exercises between American and Indonesian armed forces in July, prelude to much larger military-to-military program it will propose to reward Indonesian government for ousting senior military officers under investigation for East Timor massacres and for imposing civilian control of military; some members of Congress and human rights groups oppose resuming military ties until senior officers are tried for East Timor abuses (M)

2000-05-24.title: Washington Loosens Restrictions on Sales of Weapons to Allies
2000-05-24.abstract: American officials say Clinton administration overhauls its controls on arms exports to friendly nations; State Sec Madeleine K Albright plans to announce steps to greatly cut red tape involved in licensing of technology sales, information sharing and joint ventures between governments and contractors (M)

2000-05-25.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-05-25.abstract: EUROPE    GERMANY: DEFENSE CHIEF QUITS -- The chief of Germany's defense staff announced his resignation the day after Defense Minister Rudolf Scharping said the army required drastic reform. Gen. Hans-Peter von Kirchbach favored fewer cuts in troops and the scale of the draft than those proposed by an independent commission. He will be replaced on July 1 by Gen. Harald Kujat, above, the current head of defense planning.   Roger Cohen (NYT)    AUSTRIA: HAIDER DEFAMED -- A Vienna court has fined a prominent political scientist, Anton Pelinka, $4,800 for defaming Jorg Haider, leader of the rightist Freedom Party. In a television interview Professor Pelinka said Mr. Haider had ''trivialized'' Nazism and made certain National Socialist positions ''more politically acceptable.'' The case was initially filed by Dieter Bohmdorfer, then Mr. Haider's lawyer and now justice minister. The International Helsinki Federation for Human Rights called the conviction ''a miscarriage of justice,'' especially in light of the justice minister's role.   Roger Cohen (NYT)

2000-05-25.title: Russia Warns Afghanistan Not to Aid Rebel Groups
2000-05-25.abstract: Russian officials threaten to bomb targets in Afghanistan if Afghanistan's ruling Taliban shelters and aids Islamic militants fighting in Chechnya and former Soviet republics in Central Asia; say they do not plan ground attack in Afghanistan; United States has warned Russia not to broaden conflicts by bombing Afghanistan (M)

2000-05-25.title: From the Trade Debate: Democracy and Economics, Idealism and Politics
2000-05-25.abstract: Excepts from remarks by Reps Richard Gephardt, J Dennis Hastert, David Bonior, Charlie Norwood and Thomas M Davis at House debate on bill to grant China permanent normal trading relations (M)

2000-05-25.title: Chinese Express Joy and Relief at Passage of Trade Bill
2000-05-25.abstract: Chinese greet House vote to grant China permanent normal trade status with joy, tempered with worries about wrenching economic changes expected as China opens its markets to outsiders; government expresses concern about conditions House attached to bill (M)

2000-05-25.title: Ruling on Immunity Puts Chile Closer to Trial of Pinochet
2000-05-25.abstract: Chilean appeals court strips Gen Augusto Pinochet of immunity, removing key obstacle to his possible trial for abuses during his 17 years as dictator; it could take years of legal wrangling before trial of ailing 84-year-old can begin; ruling shows courts will take independent stands after decades of subservience to military and Pres Ricardo Lagos will let mechanisms of justice go forward despite friction with military and threats by congressional conservatives to bottle up much of his program if Pinochet prosecution continues; photo (M)

2000-05-26.title: U.S. Defends Antimissile Plan
2000-05-26.abstract: Pentagon defends its antimissile plan against Theodore A Postol, critic who contends that proposed system of ground-based interceptors is crippled by technical flaw; Postal, in May 11 letter to White House, claims Pentagon conspired to cover up test that proved system does not work (S)

2000-05-26.title: Yevgeny Khrunov, 67, Russian Astronaut
2000-05-26.abstract: Yevgeny Vasilevich Khrunov, early Russian astronaut, dies at age of 67 (S)

2000-05-26.title: Ex-G.I. in A.P. Account Concedes He Didn't See Korea Massacre
2000-05-26.abstract: Associated Press reports that Edward L Daily, Korean War veteran whose unsettling recollections of gunning down civilians made him most sought-after witness to killings, now agrees that he was not on the scene; Daily's admission comes after reporters for military affairs Web site Stripes.com and US News & World Report publish evidence raising doubts about his presence on scene; Associated Press says it remains confident of central findings of its story of killings, which won Pulitzer Prize; Daily is one of three soldiers who spoke of getting direct order to shoot civilians; such orders, if they were given, would be likely to constitute war crime, and Defense Dept has set up commission to look into matter(M)

2000-05-26.title: As Protests Grow Violent, Peru Says Runoff Will Go On
2000-05-26.abstract: Government of Pres Alberto K Fujimori says runoff presidential election would be held on May 28 as scheduled; decision increases possibility of economic sanctions against Peru for ignoring recommendations of Clinton administration, Organization of American States and several European and Latin American governments to postpone elections to ensure clean ballot count; within minutes of government announcement, violent clashes resume, with protesters setting fire to balcony of presidential palace in Lima and hurling stones at police, who respond with tear gas; photo (M)

2000-05-26.title: Court Orders Venezuela to Postpone Election on Sunday
2000-05-26.abstract: Venezuela's Supreme Court orders postponement of election scheduled for May 28, saying 'reliability and transparency' of electoral process could not be guaranteed; surprise ruling is rendered after National Electoral Council concedes that it could not ensure accurate, timely and fair vote count; no new date is set for balloting; court orders that all campaign activities be suspended until Congress, which Pres Hugo Chavez controls, chooses date; polls show Chavez leading his main opponent and former comrade in arms, Francisco Arias Cardenas, by comfortable margin (M)

2000-05-27.title: Ulster Unionists in Crucial Vote Today on Accord
2000-05-27.abstract: Members of 870-member ruling council of Ulster Unionist party convene in Belfast on May 27 for crucial vote that will either give new life to Northern Ireland peace accord or likely finish it off (M)

2000-05-27.title: House Vote on China Trade: The Politics Was Local
2000-05-27.abstract: News analysis of United States' House 237-to-197 approval of China trade deal, most important foreign policy vote so far this year; vote seen reflecting reality that members vote their districts, calculating impact of legislation on jobs gained and lost (M)

2000-05-27.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-05-27.abstract: ASIA    JAPAN: PREMIER APOLOGIZES -- Prime Minister Yoshiro Mori apologized for remarks last week that seemed to endorse emperor worship. After several days of heavy criticism, Mr. Mori, left, expressed regret in a tense news conference that his remarks had caused misunderstanding, but declined to retract his statement altogether. In his original remarks, which critics said echoed Japan's prewar militarism, Mr. Mori said Japan was ''a divine nation with the emperor at its core.''    Howard French (NYT)    SRI LANKA: MILITARY PLEA -- The government asked retired officers and policemen to re-enlist in the armed forces in hopes of bolstering 30,000 troops under assault from separatist ethnic Tamils in the northern Jaffna Peninsula. Those who rejoined would take the noncombat positions of younger soldiers, who would then be shifted to the front lines. The rebels threatened a bloodbath for soldiers who failed to surrender by today.      Celia W. Dugger (NYT)

2000-05-27.title: Senator Frees Up Funds for U.N. Forces in Kosovo
2000-05-27.abstract: Sen Judd Gregg, New Hampshire Republican, releases his hold on $40 million in State Dept funds appropriated for United Nations peacekeeping mission in Kosovo (S)

2000-05-27.title: Rally in Belgrade Protests Early Closing of Serbian Universities
2000-05-27.abstract: Several thousand students, teachers and parents rally to protest closing of universities week early by government of Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic; photo (M)

2000-05-28.title: Opposition Rally in Belgrade Draws Relatively Low Turnout
2000-05-28.abstract: Only 10,000 people show up for anti-government rally in Belgrade; relatively low turnout reflects popular disappointment with leaders of political opposition; Vuk Draskovic, head of main opposition party, delivers wandering speech; he calls for early democratic elections but he is seemingly unable to articulate clear strategy to alleviate Serbs' feeling of despair and helplessness in face of power of Pres Slobodan Milosevic; opposition officials say they will concentrate more on meetings and retail politics in countryside, where feelings run higher than in Belgrade; opposition politicians are criticized for their disunity and squabbling (M)

2000-05-28.title: Women Seek Louder Voice as World Peacemakers
2000-05-28.abstract: Effort is under way to give women greater role in peacemaking and in rebuilding communities that have been shattered by brutality of war and warlords; Ergibe Boyd, Eritrean-born American diplomat, plans to assemble women from all over Africa, as well as Middle East, Northern Ireland and Balkans, to build strategies for making their voices heard in national capitals, international organizations and summit meeting of world's seven leading industrial powers and Russia; photo (M)

2000-05-28.title: Mexico's Opposition Candidate Rebounds
2000-05-28.abstract: Vicente Fox Quesada, Mexico's leading opposition presidential candidate, gives vigorous but level-headed preformance in televised debate with two rivals that helps him recover from recent embarrassing mistakes in his campaign; opinion polls show Fox in dead heat with Francisco Labastida Ochoa; Cuauhtemoc Cardenas Solorzano is distant third (M)

2000-05-28.title: A Battle Brews as Iran's Reformers Take Over Parliament
2000-05-28.abstract: Iran's new reformer-dominated Parliament opens; it is first since Islamic revolution to include majority of members who appear eager and ready to challenge conservative clergy's dictates over daily life; difficulties facing young reform movement are visible in chamber, where only 249 of 290 seats are filled; empty seats represent districts where conservative Council of Guardians has not certified election results or voided victories of reform candidates; machinations over composition of new Parliament reflect broader conflict between entrenched religious conservatives and impatient pro-democracy movement supporting Pres Mohammad Khatami; Khatami accomplished little in way of legislation in previous Parliament, which was controlled by conservatives (M)

2000-05-28.title: After 40 Years, Israel May Be Losing Its Outsider Status at U.N.
2000-05-28.abstract: Israel is invited to join Western European regional group in United Nations, ending its virtual disenfranchisement in organization; until now, voting bloc of Arab and third world nations has isolated Israelis in General Assembly and other bodies; United States has lobbied hard to get Israel into Western Europe group as start; Israel's real goal is membership in Asian group (M)

2000-05-29.title: A Ceaseless War Leaves Trail of Death in Eritrea
2000-05-29.abstract: Ceaseless war between Ethiopia and Eritrea, which has left trail of death in Eritrea, grows even more complex as Ethiopia continues to push southward; photos; map; Ethiopian officials make no secret of desire to punish Eritrea , and say they will not end war until final peace accord is signed and Eritrea has left all land claimed by Ethiopia(M)

2000-05-29.title: U.S. Report Says the U.N. Has Improved With Changes
2000-05-29.abstract: GAO gives Congress surprisingly positive report on changes at UN, crediting Sec Gen Kofi Annan with making considerable progress on improving management; report to Senate Foreign Relations Committee differentiates between reform goals that secretary general can achieve alone, and those dependent on decisions by 188 member nations (S)

2000-05-29.title: Boundaries on Nazi Sites Remain Unsettled in Internet's Global Village
2000-05-29.abstract: PARIS—  Two French court decisions last week cast new attention on a thorny problem that continues to dog the Internet: how to enforce national laws against Nazi speech across the borderless World Wide Web.

2000-05-29.title: FUJIMORI IS VICTOR IN PERU'S RUNOFF AS PROTESTS GROW
2000-05-29.abstract: Pres Alberto K Fujimori sweeps to victory in runoff election in Peru, against opponent, Alejandro Toledo, who urged supporters to abstain because of alleged vote fraud and irregularities; hollow victory leaves country badly polarized and facing possible economic sanctions as it tries to emerge from recession; balloting caps campaign of dirty tricks, violent demonstrations and condemnations; Toledo tells rally he plans nonstop effort to nullify elections, because 'dictatorship degrades people'; he then introduces retired general Francisco Morales Bermudez, Peru's last military president, who denounces 'shameful' election; possible US sanctions discussed (M)

2000-05-29.title: Israelis Fire Into Protesters at Lebanon Border, Wounding 4
2000-05-29.abstract: Israeli soldiers fire on Lebanese and Palestinians throwing stones and trying to push across border; four people are wounded; photo (S)

2000-05-30.title: Israeli Judge Delays Ruling on 2 Abducted Guerrilla Leaders
2000-05-30.abstract: Israeli Court delays decision on whether to release two abducted Lebanese guerrilla leaders whose freedom has been demanded by Hezbollah as condition for halting anti-Israeli hostilities; will rule on July 12 on cases of Sheik Abdel Karim Obeid of Hezbollah, who was seized in 1989, and Mustafa al- Dirani of Amal movement who was seized in 1994; both were forcibly taken in Lebanon and have been used as bargaining chips for release of Capt Ron Arad, Israeli airman whose plane was lost over Lebanon in 1986 (M)

2000-05-30.title: WORLD BRIEFING
2000-05-30.abstract: MIDDLE EAST    ISRAEL: BARAK BACKS PERES -- Shimon Peres, a former prime minister, was endorsed by Prime Minister Ehud Barak to succeed President Ezer Weizman, who is resigning in the wake of a corruption scandal. In a secret ballot in Parliament scheduled for July 31, Mr. Peres, left, who now holds the regional cooperation portfolio in Mr. Barak's cabinet, is expected to face Moshe Katzav of the opposition Likud Party.   William A. Orme, Jr. (NYT)    ISRAEL: LESBIANS' LANDMARK VICTORY -- An Israeli-American lesbian couple won the right here to register as the two mothers of their 4-year-old son, Matan, provoking outrage from the religious right. Nicole Berner-Kadish adopted Matan, the biological son of her partner, Ruti Berner-Kadish, in California. But Israel fought their petition to have Matan recognized as Nicole's child until the High Court, in a groundbreaking ruling for gay couples, said a child may indeed have two mothers.   Deborah Sontag (NYT)

2000-05-30.title: News Analysis; Peru Election Turmoil: Stability vs. Democracy
2000-05-30.abstract: News analysis of controversy and issues surrounding Peru's Presidential election in which Pres Alberto Fujimori won third consecutive term in vote international observers say was fixed, and from which opposition candidate Alejandro Toledo withdrew in protest; photo (M)

2000-05-30.title: Painful Glimpse of Home for Palestinians
2000-05-30.abstract: Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon is bittersweet experience for Palestinian refugees who have lived in squalid, supposedly makeshift camps there for nearly half century; their goal of returning to West Bank homeland remains as elusive as ever since many feel abandoned by Palestinian leadership in Gaza City; even if peace agreement is reached and Palestinian state declared, mass migration of millions of refugees to Palestinian-ruled West Bank and Gaza, as well as some parts of Israel under family reunification policy, is seen as extremely unlikely and issue is likely to be deferred; maps; photos (M)

2000-05-30.title: Japan's Employers Are Giving Bonuses For Having Babies
2000-05-30.abstract: Japanese companies are offering employees bonuses for having babies as nation struggles to reverse record low birthrates that pose many long-term problems; most initiatives focus on making it easier and more economical to raise children, extending flexible work hours, family leave and child care; some pay cash and monthly subsidies; average birthrate for Japanese woman in 1998 was 1.38 children, one of lowest in world; $10,000 bonus being offered employees by Bandai Co for every baby born after their second child discussed; photo (M)

2000-05-31.title: First Results From Haitian Election Show Aristide Gaining Senate
2000-05-31.abstract: Preliminary results indicate party of Jean-Bertrand Aristide, former president of Haiti, gained firm control of Haitian Senate in May 21 election; party gained 14 of 19 Senate seats in first parliamentary election since 1997 (M)

2000-05-31.title: Hezbollah Offers a Helping Hand in Southern Lebanon
2000-05-31.abstract: Hezbollah arrives in southern Lebanon in force following Israeli withdrawal; is operating as kind of parallel government offering social services, development loans and reconstruction aid; photo; map (M)(

2000-05-31.title: U.S. Retreats On Peru Vote; Assessment Is Not 'Final'
2000-05-31.abstract: Clinton administration backs away from its own harsh assessment that presidential runoff election in Peru was invalid; State Dept reiterates 'regret' that Pres Alberto K Fujimori decided to proceed with election despite allegations from international monitors that it fairness could not be guaranteed (M)

2000-05-31.title: China in Campaign to Expel Koreans Who Enter Illegally
2000-05-31.abstract: China steps up efforts to expel North Korean immigrants who have sneaked in illegally to escape famine in homeland; formerly turned blind eye to estimated 100,000 to 200,000 living in border region who face persecution as traitors if they return home; begins enforcing longstanding policy that North Koreans are economic migrants who must leave; experts cite China's hope for visit from North Korean leader Kim Jong Il and fear that steady stream of refugees could overwhelm resources; UN refugee agency expresses concern; says 'food migrants' probably meet criteria for protected political refugee status, which would make their return illegal under international law; Western nations, though disturbed, are silent; both sides fear criticism could prompt China to seal border, closing what remains of important safety valve for five-year famine experts say has caused as many as two million deaths; map; photo (M)

2000-05-31.title: Clinton Visits Europe With Same Baggage, Trade and Missiles, That Reagan Carried
2000-05-31.abstract: Pres Clinton arrives in Portugal on first leg of his final European trip, becoming first president to visit country since Ronald Reagan in 1985; finds himself faced with same major issues of trade and missile defense; is warmly greeted by Portuguese Pres Jorge Sampaio at state dinner; his itinerary; map (M)
